articles.title: Heatwave alert: Experts advise safety measures - Rising Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Srinagar, Jul 05: With temperatures soaring across Kashmir, public health experts have urged people to take adequate precautions against heat-related illnesses, warning that prolonged exposure to high temperatures can lead to dehydration, heat exhaustion and even life-threatening heat stroke.
Dr. S. Muhammad Salim Khan, Professor, Department of Community Medicine, GMC Srinagar said Kashmir summers are becoming increasingly hotter, with high temperatures and strong sunlight posing serious health risks.
“Simple preventive measures adopted today can help avoid severe medical complications tomorrow. People have been advised to drink 2.5 to 3.5 litres of water daily, without waiting until they feel thirsty,” he said.
As per health experts, those engaged in outdoor work or experiencing excessive sweating should consume Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) to replace lost fluids and electrolytes.
